capacity_guard.behavior: Candidates below the absolute improvement floor remain JSON-only and do not write final checkpoint NPZ artifacts.

assessment: Followup200 adds a capacity-safe checkpoint floor and proves it on the retained followup190 frontier. The component-block frame:rx diagnostic again finds only a below-floor 1.40e-7 N improvement and writes no checkpoint. A one-factor current-tangent global Krylov guard no-descends: its best trial worsens the residual to 0.0345169949 N. The retained continuation frontier therefore remains followup190, while future G1 probes should require material descent before writing large NPZ state.
recommended_next_action: Keep --min-checkpoint-improvement-inf-n at or above the weak-step floor for all exploratory cached-JVP probes.
Do not resume blind top96/top128 or the tested current-tangent global Krylov factor from followup190.
Next useful work is a new block-aware trust/globalization formulation that changes direction construction, not more replay of the same monolithic LS or global Krylov direction.
